message.content:
Run CODEX (GPT-5.6) to gate Phase 3A batch-2 docstrings. Write /tmp/doc_b2_gate.md then 'cd /tmp && timeout 200 codex exec --skip-git-repo-check - < /tmp/doc_b2_gate.md 2>&1 | tail -25' (embed inline; medium effort; no sandbox flags).
Evidence: doc={"docstrings_added":51,"files_changed":14,"skipped_loadbearing":[],"collection_after":"16738 tests collected, 11 errors (16738/11)","claims_accurate":true}; validate={"ast_equivalence_all_pass":true,"ast_failures":[],"no_loadbearing_confirmed":true,"oracle_matches":true,"oracle_sha":"9c0a3d438da943242c98e2fe714494c342d42d02102202d75a61a4554339db98","collection_ok":true}. Claims: docstring-stripped ASTs identical to HEAD (docstrings-only), per-symbol __doc__-audit finds no load-bearing docstring, oracle replay byte-identical (9c0a3d438da943242c98e2fe714494c342d42d02102202d75a61a4554339db98), all Raises/return claims verified accurate.
Ask codex: "Is this a provably zero-behavior-change docstrings-only batch, safe to ship? Any inaccurate Raises/return-shape claim, or any load-bearing __doc__ among those added? End with DOCS-SAFE or DOCS-REVISE (+ the one blocking item if REVISE)."
Return verdict + points.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- artifact-design: Design guidance and fundamentals for Artifacts.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.text: I'll write the gate file and run CODEX to evaluate the docstrings batch.

input.content:
You are gating a "docstrings-only" code change batch (Phase 3A, batch 2) for safety to ship. This batch claims to add docstrings ONLY, with provably zero behavior change.

## What "safe to ship" requires
1. Zero behavior change: after stripping docstrings, the ASTs must be identical to HEAD (i.e., the only changes are string literals in docstring position).
2. No load-bearing docstrings: no added `__doc__` is read at runtime (e.g., used by argparse help, `--help` text, `__doc__`-driven dispatch, doctest execution, CLI help, pydantic/Field descriptions, or any code that inspects `.__doc__`).
3. Accurate claims: any `Raises:` / return-shape / return-type statements written in the new docstrings must be factually accurate (not misleading).

## Evidence provided

Documentation agent report:
```json
{"docstrings_added":51,"files_changed":14,"skipped_loadbearing":[],"collection_after":"16738 tests collected, 11 errors (16738/11)","claims_accurate":true}
```

Independent validation agent report:
```json
{"ast_equivalence_all_pass":true,"ast_failures":[],"no_loadbearing_confirmed":true,"oracle_matches":true,"oracle_sha":"9c0a3d438da943242c98e2fe714494c342d42d02102202d75a61a4554339db98","collection_ok":true}
```

Claims to evaluate:
- Docstring-stripped ASTs are identical to HEAD (docstrings-only change, verified across all 14 files).
- Per-symbol `__doc__` audit finds no load-bearing docstring (none of the 51 added docstrings are read at runtime).
- Oracle replay is byte-identical to baseline (oracle_sha 9c0a3d438da943242c98e2fe714494c342d42d02102202d75a61a4554339db98).
- All `Raises:` / return-shape claims in the new docstrings were verified accurate.
- Test collection after the change: 16738 tests collected with 11 errors (matching the pre-existing baseline; no new errors introduced).

## Your task
Answer: Is this a provably zero-behavior-change docstrings-only batch, safe to ship? Specifically:
- Do the AST-equivalence + oracle byte-identity + collection-parity evidence establish zero behavior change?
- Is there any inaccurate `Raises:`/return-shape claim, or any load-bearing `__doc__` among those added, that the evidence would fail to catch?
- Flag any gap in the verification methodology (e.g., a runtime `__doc__` consumer that AST-strip + oracle-replay would not detect).

Keep it concise. End your answer with exactly one line: either `DOCS-SAFE` or `DOCS-REVISE`. If REVISE, add the single blocking item on that same line.

content.content: File created successfully at: /tmp/doc_b2_gate.md (file state is current in your context — no need to Read it back)

**Assessing docstring impact evidence gaps**
**Flagging incomplete independent docstring audit**
**Identifying runtime docstring read risks**
codex
AST equivalence proves the edits are docstring-only, but oracle identity and collection parity prove only exercised paths—not universal zero behavior change. A dynamic or unexercised consumer (`getattr`, `inspect.getdoc`, `help`, framework/plugin reflection, doctest) could escape both. The evidence also asserts, rather than independently demonstrates, the accuracy of `Raises:` and return-shape claims.

DOCS-REVISE: Provide an exhaustive independent audit covering dynamic/unexercised `__doc__` consumers and validating every behavioral docstring claim.
tokens used
3,711
AST equivalence proves the edits are docstring-only, but oracle identity and collection parity prove only exercised paths—not universal zero behavior change. A dynamic or unexercised consumer (`getattr`, `inspect.getdoc`, `help`, framework/plugin reflection, doctest) could escape both. The evidence also asserts, rather than independently demonstrates, the accuracy of `Raises:` and return-shape claims.

DOCS-REVISE: Provide an exhaustive independent audit covering dynamic/unexercised `__doc__` consumers and validating every behavioral docstring claim.

codex_points: AST equivalence proves the edits are docstring-only (only string literals in docstring position changed), which is solid evidence for zero source-behavior change.
However, oracle byte-identity and collection parity only cover EXERCISED code paths -- they do not establish universal zero behavior change for dynamic or unexercised consumers.

A dynamic or unexercised __doc__ consumer (getattr, inspect.getdoc, help(), framework/plugin reflection, doctest execution) could read an added docstring and change behavior without tripping AST-strip or oracle-replay.
The Raises:/return-shape accuracy is ASSERTED by the agents rather than independently demonstrated in the evidence provided.

Blocking item (single): provide an exhaustive independent audit covering dynamic/unexercised __doc__ consumers AND independently validating every behavioral (Raises:/return-shape) docstring claim, rather than relying on agent self-report + exercised-path oracle.
